title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description:
Given a string `s`, return the longest palindromic substring in `s`.

 

Example 1:

Input: s = "babad"
Output: "bab"
Explanation: "aba" is also a valid answer.

Example 2:

Input: s = "cbbd"
Output: "bb"
constraints: `s` consist of only digits and English letters.

hint_plan.level_1: How can we reuse a previously computed palindrome to compute a larger palindrome?
hint_plan.level_2: If “aba” is a palindrome, is “xabax” a palindrome? Similarly is “xabay” a palindrome?
hint_plan.level_3:
Complexity based hint:
If we use brute-force and check whether for every start and end position a substring is a palindrome we have O(n^2) start - end pairs and O(n) palindromic checks. Can we reduce the time for palindromic checks to O(1) by reusing some previous computation.
hint_plan.level_4:
Complexity based hint:
If we use brute-force and check whether for every start and end position a substring is a palindrome we have O(n^2) start - end pairs and O(n) palindromic checks. Can we reduce the time for palindromic checks to O(1) by reusing some previous computation.

canonical_solution_summary:
Complexity based hint:
If we use brute-force and check whether for every start and end position a substring is a palindrome we have O(n^2) start - end pairs and O(n) palindromic checks. Can we reduce the time for palindromic checks to O(1) by reusing some previous computation.
hints_per_sentence: Start by defining the function with the given parameter.
Check if the input string is empty to handle edge cases.
Return an empty string if the input is empty.
Store the length of the string in a variable for later use.
Initialize a 2D list (matrix) to store palindrome status for substrings.
Set a variable to track the starting index of the longest palindrome found.
Set a variable to track the length of the longest palindrome found.
Loop through each index to handle single-character substrings.
Mark each single character as a palindrome in the matrix.
Loop through adjacent pairs of indices to handle two-character substrings.
Check if two adjacent characters are equal.
If equal, mark them as a palindrome and update the start and max length.
Now handle substrings longer than two characters by iterating over possible lengths.
For each length, loop through possible starting indices within the string.
Calculate the ending index based on the starting index and length.
Check if the outer characters match and the inner substring is a palindrome.
If true, mark this substring as a palindrome and update if it's the longest found.
Finally, return the substring from the start index with the maximum length.
final_explanation: This solution uses dynamic programming to efficiently find the longest palindromic substring. It builds a table where each entry indicates whether a substring is a palindrome, starting from single characters and expanding to longer substrings by checking outer characters and inner substrings. By storing results, it avoids redundant checks and achieves O(n^2) time complexity with O(n^2) space, ensuring all possible substrings are considered to identify the longest palindrome.
